description: Add missing unit tests for critical MCP server modules: tool dispatch pipeline (security-sensitive sanitization, structured errors, Zod validation) and analyze-site suggestion generation functions (pure functions with zero coverage).

userStories.title: Add unit tests for mcp-tool-dispatch.ts pure functions
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want unit tests for the pure functions in mcp-tool-dispatch.ts so that security-sensitive logic (prototype pollution prevention) and error formatting are verified.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover sanitizeOutput: primitives passthrough, nested objects, arrays, __proto__/__constructor__/prototype key removal, depth limit at 50, null/undefined passthrough
Tests cover formatStructuredError: code-only format, with category, with retryable, with retryAfterMs, all fields present, no structured fields (legacy format)
Tests cover formatZodError: single issue, multiple issues, root-level issue, nested path
Tests cover truncateParamsPreview: short args passthrough, truncation at 200 chars with ellipsis
userStories.notes: The functions to test are in platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-tool-dispatch.ts. They are currently not exported — you will need to export the pure functions (sanitizeOutput, formatStructuredError, formatZodError, truncateParamsPreview) for testing. sanitizeOutput (line 34) removes __proto__, constructor, prototype keys recursively with depth limit of 50. formatStructuredError (line 105) builds human-readable + JSON block error messages. formatZodError (line 133) formats ZodError issues. truncateParamsPreview (line 92) truncates JSON to 200 chars. Follow the existing test patterns in the package — see platform/mcp-server/src/sanitize-error.test.ts or platform/mcp-server/src/permissions.test.ts for style reference. Use @opentabs-dev/shared's test utilities if any are available. Use a tagged export pattern (export for testing) or re-export via a test helper if you prefer not to pollute the public API.

userStories.title: Add unit tests for handleBrowserToolCall and handlePluginToolCall
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want unit tests for the browser tool and plugin tool dispatch handlers so that permission evaluation, confirmation flow, concurrency limiting, and error handling are verified.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover handleBrowserToolCall: disabled tool returns error, Zod validation failure, permission denied, permission allow (skips confirmation), confirm flow (allow/deny/always), successful execution with output sanitization, audit entry recording

Tests cover handlePluginToolCall: concurrency limit (MAX_CONCURRENT_DISPATCHES_PER_PLUGIN=5) rejection, JSON schema validation failure, dispatch error handling, structured error propagation (ToolError with code/category/retryable/retryAfterMs), successful dispatch with progress reporting, audit entry recording on success and failure
Mock dispatchToExtension, sendConfirmationRequest, sendInvocationStart, sendInvocationEnd via vi.mock
Tests are in platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-tool-dispatch.test.ts (same file as US-001)
userStories.notes: handleBrowserToolCall starts at line 159 and handlePluginToolCall is further down in platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-tool-dispatch.ts. These handlers require mocking: dispatchToExtension (from extension-protocol.ts), sendConfirmationRequest, sendInvocationStart, sendInvocationEnd, evaluatePermission (from permissions.ts), isBrowserToolEnabled and appendAuditEntry (from state.ts). Create a minimal mock ServerState with the required fields. For handlePluginToolCall, test the concurrency limiter by setting up activeDispatches Map entries. The handlers are complex — use opus for this story. Export handleBrowserToolCall and handlePluginToolCall if they aren't already exported. See platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-setup-dispatch.test.ts for patterns on testing dispatch logic.

userStories.title: Add unit tests for analyze-site suggestion generation functions
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want unit tests for the pure suggestion generation functions in the analyze-site module so that tool suggestion quality is verified independently of browser interaction.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover generateSuggestions: REST endpoints produce suggestions, GraphQL endpoints produce query suggestions, tRPC endpoints produce procedure suggestions, form detection produces form suggestions, WebSocket detection produces WS suggestions, empty analysis returns empty suggestions
Tests cover restEndpointSuggestion: various HTTP methods map to correct verbs, path segments extracted for tool naming
Tests cover graphqlQuerySuggestions: extracts operation names from request body samples, handles missing/malformed bodies
Tests cover extractTrpcProcedure: extracts procedure name from tRPC URLs, returns undefined for non-tRPC URLs
Tests cover deriveFormName: derives name from action URL, falls back to field patterns
Tests cover extractResourceName: extracts resource name from URL path, handles edge cases
Tests cover httpMethodToVerb: maps GET/POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE to correct verbs
Tests cover toSnakeCase and capitalizeFirst: string utility edge cases
userStories.notes: The functions to test are in platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/analyze-site/index.ts. These are pure functions: generateSuggestions (line 559), restEndpointSuggestion (line 649), graphqlQuerySuggestions (line 668), extractTrpcProcedure (line 696), deriveFormName (line 707), extractPathSegments (line 731), extractResourceName (line 740), httpMethodToVerb (line 755), toSnakeCase (line 772), capitalizeFirst (line 779). They are not currently exported — export them for testing. The detection module tests (detect-apis.test.ts, detect-auth.test.ts, etc.) in the same directory show the test patterns to follow. The functions take typed inputs (ApiEndpoint, ApiAnalysis, AuthAnalysis, etc.) and return ToolSuggestion objects. Import the types from the detection module files.
